routing_logic.description: Internal routing state - checks what needs acknowledging
rules: IF swamp_complete AND NOT mayor_acknowledged_swamp_complete -> swamp_complete_first_time
IF ruins_complete AND NOT mayor_acknowledged_ruins_complete -> ruins_complete_first_time
IF refugee_complete AND NOT mayor_acknowledged_refugee_complete -> refugee_complete_first_time
IF mine_complete AND NOT mayor_acknowledged_mine_complete -> mine_complete_first_time
IF all_acknowledged AND NOT all_complete -> multiple_progress

introduction: *The Mayor looks up from his desk, worry etched on his face.*
Any news? Every hour counts.
The tremors are getting worse.
Investigate the Swamp Church, the Hill Ruins, or the Refugee Camp.
Find my family. Find out what is happening to Redstone.

options.text: Remind me where I should look.
The tremors are definitely getting worse.

introduction: Three locations demand investigation:
The Swamp Church - old chapel in the marsh, reports of lights.
The Hill Ruins - ancient stones where people vanish.
The Refugee Camp - displaced miners with strange dreams.
Start wherever you think best.
*He slams his fist on the desk.*
Started weeks ago. Getting stronger every day.
Miners say it is the old shafts settling.
But the timing... right when people vanish?
I do not believe in coincidences.
Find what is taking our people.
Maybe that will stop the tremors too.
*His face pales as you describe what you found.*
A cult? Operating right under our noses?
Kidnapping our people for... for rituals?
*He steadies himself against the desk.*
This is worse than I feared.
Thank you for uncovering this.
*He listens intently to your report.*
A portal? Some kind of... dimensional gateway?
And it is sealed? Locked?
*He runs his hand through his hair.*
Whatever is beyond that door took my family.
We need to find a way through.
*He takes the key from you, examining it closely.*
This... this is it. The key to the ruins.
Those poor people were guarding it all along.
*He looks up with grim determination.*
Now we can reach the source of this corruption.
*He examines the ore samples you recovered.*
This is what Henrik sealed away all those years ago.
Strange energy... unnatural glow...
*He sets them down carefully.*
This ore must be connected to the portal somehow.
Keep investigating.
The more we know, the better our chances.
Let me think about what we know...
*He reviews his notes.*

options.text: Never mind, I'll figure it out.

introduction: The Swamp Church is still a mystery.
If there is cult activity there, we need to know.
The Hill Ruins are the key to this whole thing.
That is where the disappearances center.
Whatever is there, we need to face it.
The Refugee Camp might have survivors.
People who escaped whatever is happening.
They might know something crucial.
*He looks more hopeful than before.*
You are making real progress.
The picture is becoming clearer.
But we still need to know everything before we act.
*He stands, resolve hardening in his eyes.*
You have done it. We know what we face.
A cult. A portal. My family taken beyond.
*He grips your shoulder.*
Now we end this.
When you are ready, take the key to the ruins.
I will gather what forces we have left.
Bring my family home.
*The Mayor's office is transformed. Sunlight streams through clean windows, and the man himself looks ten years younger.*
*He stands as you enter, a broad smile on his face*
The hero of Redstone returns!
*He comes around the desk to grip your shoulders*
My family is safe. The town is saved. The Shadow Blight is sealed.
We owe you everything.

options.text: How is your family? Are they recovering?
I just did what needed to be done.

introduction: *His smile falters slightly*
They're alive. That's what matters most.
But they were... touched by the corruption. Briefly.
The healers say they'll recover fully, but they have nightmares.
Dreams of darkness and whispering shadows.
*He steadies himself*
But they're HERE. Safe. Healing.
That's more than I dared hope for when you first walked into the Broken Blade.
*He shakes his head*
Don't diminish what you did.
You walked into the deepest darkness this region has ever known.
You faced corrupted cultists, ancient horrors, and the very essence of the Shadow Blight itself.
*His voice grows serious*
And you came back. With my family. With survivors.
That's not 'just doing your job.' That's heroism.
*He walks to the window, looking out over the town square*
The portal is destroyed. The ritual interrupted. Vexthar is dead.
But the portal-stone itself still exists in those ruins.
The Aethel left their mark on this land centuries ago.
*He turns back to you*
We must remain vigilant. The Shadow Blight may sleep, but it does not die.
Henrik is organizing a watch. We'll seal the ruins permanently.
And we'll remember. We'll teach our children what happened here.
So it never happens again.
*He gestures broadly*
Rebuilding. Healing. Living.
The refugees can return to their homes. The mine can reopen - carefully.
Trade will resume. The Broken Blade will be full again.
*A genuine smile*
Redstone will be a town of life again, not fear.
And we have you to thank for that.
*He produces a heavy coin purse*
This is from the town treasury. Your reward.
It's not enough. It could never be enough.
But it's what we can offer.
*He also hands you an ornate key*
And this. The Key to Redstone. An honorary citizenship.
You will always have a home here. Always be welcome.
You are family now.
*The Mayor nods warmly*
Is there anything else I can help you with?

options.text: Nothing right now. Good day, Mayor.

introduction: *His expression softens*
Better each day. The nightmares are fading.
My daughter even smiled yesterday. Really smiled.
It will take time, but we have time now. Thanks to you.
Remarkably well. People are resilient.
The refugees have returned. Businesses are reopening.
Henrik sealed the mine entrance - permanently this time.
We're building a memorial. For those we lost.
Redstone is healing.
*He nods solemnly.*
Go with my blessing.
May the gods watch over you.
Save Redstone.
*The Mayor embraces his family, tears streaming*
You did it. You brought them back.
*He turns to you, his voice thick with emotion*
Words cannot express my gratitude. Redstone owes you everything.
*He presses a pouch of gold and a ring into your hands*
Take these. You've earned far more than I can ever repay.
*The Mayor's joy is tempered by profound loss*
You saved those you could. I am grateful for that.
But the empty chairs at my table... they will haunt me forever.
*He steadies himself, forcing composure*
Take this. You earned it, even if the cost was high.
*The Mayor stands alone, hollow-eyed*
They're gone. All of them.
*His voice cracks*
You sealed the portal. You saved the town. But my family...
*He cannot continue*
